summary: Fixed the root cause of Google Docs tabs being dropped from their group: when a grouped tab's URL drifts in-page (e.g. Google Docs rewriting its ?tab= query via the History API), the group label's stored urlKey now heals to follow the live URL so downstream exact-key comparisons keep matching. Also added durable, runtime-toggleable grouping diagnostics that survive MV3 service-worker restarts.

changes.title: Heal drifted label urlKey on an in-page URL change
changes.description: Added the mirror case to the onUpdated in-page branch: when a grouped tab changes URL without navigating (same origin+pathname), the group label's stale urlKey is located by page identity (samePageKey) and rewritten to the live getUrlKey(changeInfo.url) — or the stale slot is dropped if the live key is already present, avoiding a duplicate. The group title is resolved from the in-memory groups map, falling back to getTabGroup after a service-worker restart. Real navigations still eject unchanged. This eliminates the recorded-key vs live-key mismatch that made the auto-grouped eject, handleActiveTabsGroupChanges, and post-restart reconciliation drop the doc.

tests.name: service_worker.js › onUpdated in-page URL change keeps tab grouped › heals the drifted label urlKey to the live key on a query-only change
tests.description: A grouped Google Docs tab whose ?tab= query changes in-page has its stale label urlKey rewritten to the live key and is not ungrouped.

example.input: label.urlKeys=[url-.../edit?tab=t.A]; onUpdated change to .../edit?tab=t.B on grouped tab 3

tests.name: service_worker.js › onUpdated in-page URL change keeps tab grouped › heals a stale slot even when it is a third query variant
tests.description: The drifted slot is located by page identity even when the recorded key is a third ?tab= variant, not the immediately-previous one.

example.input: label.urlKeys=[url-.../edit?tab=t.A]; previous live key ?tab=t.B; onUpdated change to ?tab=t.C

tests.name: service_worker.js › onUpdated in-page URL change keeps tab grouped › removes the stale slot without duplicating an already-present live key
tests.description: When the live key is already recorded in the label, healing removes the stale slot instead of creating a duplicate.

tests.name: service_worker.js › onUpdated in-page URL change keeps tab grouped › does not heal the label on a real navigation
tests.description: A real navigation to a different path still ejects the tab and leaves the label untouched — heal only fires on an in-page change.

example.output: ungroup called with tab 3; label.urlKeys unchanged [url-https://example.com/a]

tests.name: service_worker.js › onUpdated in-page URL change keeps tab grouped › heals via the getTabGroup fallback when the groups map is cold
tests.description: When the in-memory groups map is cold (service worker just restarted), the label title is resolved via getTabGroup and the drifted key is still healed.

example.input: groups map empty; getTabGroup(5)->{title:Work}; label.urlKeys=[url-.../edit?tab=t.A]; change to ?tab=t.B

changes.title: Durable, runtime-toggleable grouping diagnostics
changes.description: Reworked debugGroup so that, beyond the optional compile-time console trace, it persists each grouping-decision breadcrumb to a capped groupingLog ring buffer in chrome.storage.local when the runtime debugGrouping flag is set — making grouping decisions inspectable after the fact and across MV3 service-worker restarts, with no source edit or reload. The pure append+trim logic lives in a new src/lib/utils/groupingLog.js helper. A breadcrumb was also added at the previously-silent navigation-eject site.

tests.name: appendGroupingLog › appends the entry to the end of a populated store
tests.description: A new breadcrumb is appended to the end of an existing trail, preserving order.

tests.name: appendGroupingLog › treats a missing store as an empty array
tests.description: A missing/undefined store is treated as an empty trail rather than throwing.

tests.name: appendGroupingLog › treats a non-array store as an empty array
tests.description: A corrupted non-array store is discarded in favor of a fresh single-entry trail.

tests.name: appendGroupingLog › trims to cap, keeping the most-recent entries
tests.description: Once the trail exceeds cap, the oldest entries fall off and only the most-recent cap survive.

tests.name: appendGroupingLog › keeps length at cap when already full
tests.description: A trail already at capacity slides forward by one on each append, never growing past cap.

tests.name: appendGroupingLog › does not mutate the input array
tests.description: The caller's stored array is not mutated; the helper returns a new array.

example.output: store still [{n:1}]; result is a different array reference

tests.name: appendGroupingLog › defaults the cap to 200 entries
tests.description: The default cap is 200, matching the worker's GROUPING_LOG_CAP.

example.input: append 250 entries n=0..249 with default cap

tests.name: service_worker.js › debugGroup › persists a breadcrumb to groupingLog when the runtime flag is on
tests.description: With debugGrouping enabled, debugGroup writes the breadcrumb into the groupingLog ring buffer via storage.local.set.

example.output: groupingLog written with length 2; last entry {event:heal, details:{tabId:7, urlKey:url-x}}

tests.name: service_worker.js › debugGroup › does not persist when the runtime flag is off
tests.description: With the runtime flag off (the default), debugGroup writes no groupingLog to storage.
